steps.title: Use cubic regression as a shortcut
steps.content: Instead of carefully combining every term by hand, evaluate the expression at four x-values and fit a cubic. Enter the source expression as a list in Desmos.

steps.title: Fit the equivalent cubic
steps.content:
Now regress those outputs against $ax^3+bx^2+cx+d$:
$$y_1\sim ax_1^3+bx_1^2+cx_1+d$$
Desmos returns $a=15$, $b=5$, $c=5$, and $d=2$.

steps.title: Confirm with algebra
steps.content:
Combining like terms gives the same result:
$$9x^3+6x^3=15x^3$$
$$5x^2\text{ stays }5x^2,\quad 5x\text{ stays }5x,\quad 7-5=2$$
So the expression is $15x^3+5x^2+5x+2$, which is choice D.
